humanities_role_explanation: Uses multiple Chinese humanities subjects (such as Chinese history, literature, art studies, and logic) as evaluation datasets within a broader multi-discipline benchmark.
benchmark_modalities_explanation: Text-only multi-choice questions in Chinese, evaluated using zero-shot/few-shot answer-only and chain-of-thought prompting.
analytical_tasks_explanation: Evaluates factual knowledge and reasoning capabilities through multiple-choice questions across four difficulty tiers (middle school, high school, college, and professional).

general_purpose_benchmark.note: Evaluates 52 diverse disciplines across STEM, Social Science, Humanities, and other professional domains.

evaluates_generative_llm.note: Evaluates models such as GPT-4, ChatGPT, Claude, LLaMA-65B, GLM-130B, ChatGLM-6B, Chinese-LLaMA, and MOSS.

llm_is_primary_system.note: Generative LLMs are the primary systems being tested on the benchmark.

arts_humanities_data.note: Evaluates subjects across History, Philosophy (Logic, Ideological and Moral Cultivation), Language and Linguistics (Chinese Language and Literature), and Visual Arts and Performing Arts (Art Studies).

quantitative_metrics.note: Uses accuracy (%) as the primary metric across all tasks and splits.

published_after_march_2023.note: Initially uploaded to arXiv in May 2023 and published in NeurIPS 2023.

english_language.note: The scientific paper is written in English, while the benchmark dataset evaluates Chinese language understanding.

summary: C-Eval is a comprehensive, multi-level Chinese evaluation suite designed to assess the advanced knowledge and reasoning abilities of foundation models. It contains 13,948 multiple-choice questions spanning 52 disciplines across middle school, high school, college, and professional levels. It also includes C-Eval Hard, a specialized subset of demanding math and science subjects designed to test advanced reasoning.
key_findings: GPT-4 was the only model to exceed a 60% average accuracy, scoring 66.4% in zero-shot and 68.7% in five-shot settings. Chinese-oriented models, such as GLM-130B, narrowed the performance gap with ChatGPT on humanities and social sciences but significantly lagged on STEM subjects and the advanced reasoning tasks in C-Eval Hard.
